title: Electrochemical methods of removing dissolved oxygen from drilling or completion fluids
abstract: A method of treating a fluid in a subterranean formation comprises injecting a fluid into the subterranean formation, the fluid containing dissolved oxygen; contacting the fluid with an oxygen removal device, the oxygen removal device comprising an anode, a cathode comprising metallic nanoparticles loaded on a support, an ion exchange membrane disposed between, and electrically separating the anode and the cathode, and a power source electrically coupled to the anode and the cathode; and reducing the amount of the dissolved oxygen in the fluid.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of treating a fluid in a subterranean formation, the method characterized by: injecting a fluid into a subterranean formation, the fluid containing dissolved oxygen; contacting the fluid with an oxygen removal device, the oxygen removal device comprising an anode (30), a cathode (50) comprising metallic nanoparticles loaded on a support, an ion exchange membrane (40) disposed between, and electrically separating the anode (30) and the cathode (50), and a power source (20) electrically coupled to the anode (30) and the cathode (50); and reducing the amount of the dissolved oxygen in the fluid. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the support comprises a carbon support or a metal oxide support or a combination thereof, the carbon support comprises activated carbon, carbon nanotubes, graphene, reduced graphene oxide, carbon fibers, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing; the metal oxide support comprises T1O2, WO3, S1O2, MnC , RuC"2, RhC , or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing; and the anode (30) comprises metals, metal oxides, metal borides, conductive polymers, semiconductors, ceramics, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the metallic nanoparticles comprise platinum, gold, copper, silver, tungsten carbide, titanium diboride, or a combination comprising at least one of the foregoing. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the cathode (50) comprises silver nanoparticles loaded on a carbon support. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the fluid is injected via a tubular (10). 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the oxygen removal device is disposed on a surface of the tubular (10). 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the anode (30) is the tubular (10). 8. The method of claim 5, wherein the anode (30) is a coating disposed on a surface of the tubular (10); and the coating has a thickness of about 500 nm to about 500 microns. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the ion exchange membrane (40) is a cation exchange membrane (40). 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the ion exchange membrane (40) is an anion exchange membrane (40). 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, further comprising applying a voltage of about 0.1 V to about 5 V to the anode (30) and the cathode (50). 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the fluid is a drilling fluid or a completion fluid. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the fluid flows through the oxygen removal device at a flow rate of about 50 gallons/min to about 500 gallons/min. 14. The method of any one of claims 5 to 8, wherein the amount of the dissolved oxygen is reduced after the fluid is flowed out of the tubular (10). 15. A method of removing dissolved oxygen in a drilling or completion fluid, the method characterized by: flowing the drilling or completion fluid through an oxygen removal device comprising an anode (30), a cathode (50) comprising metallic nanoparticles loaded on a support, an ion exchange membrane (40) disposed between, and electrically separating the cathode (50) and the anode (30) cathode (50), and a power source (20) electrically coupled to the anode (30) and the cathode (50). wherein optionally the dissolved oxygen is removed before the drilling or completion fluid is injected into a subterranean formation.
